mismatchDiagnostics.note: Buckets apply only to subset-eligible rows (parsable gold label + ≥1 model claim). `gold_text_wrong_position`: some claim has exact gold `text` but no claim has gold `position_in_source` — very common when **gold positions are document-level** while each eval row is a **single-sentence `input`**, so the model invents a local index (1, 2, …) that cannot match the label. `gold_position_wrong_text`: some claim matches gold position but not gold text (often paraphrase; see EXTRACTION_SYSTEM). `split_across_claims`: exact text on one claim and gold position on another.

mismatchSamples.goldText: The Christian faith benefits from a profound engagement with philosophy and science.

claimsPreview.text: The Christian faith benefits from a profound engagement with philosophy and science.

mismatchSamples.goldText: The Summa theologiae represents Aquinas’s most considered thought on a given topic.

claimsPreview.text: The Summa theologiae represents Aquinas’s most considered thought on a given topic.

mismatchSamples.goldText: A careful study of Aquinas’s work requires comparing multiple treatments of the same issue across different texts.

claimsPreview.text: A careful study of Aquinas’s work requires comparing multiple treatments of the same issue across different texts.

mismatchSamples.goldText: The scholastic disputed-question format is a method that lends itself to argumentative rigor.

claimsPreview.text: The scholastic disputed-question format is a method that lends itself to argumentative rigor.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Philosophy is important for a proper understanding of theology.

claimsPreview.text: Philosophy is important for a proper understanding of theology.

mismatchSamples.goldText: If a philosophical claim is contrary to the Christian faith, it is an abuse of philosophy resulting from a failure of reason.

claimsPreview.text: If a philosophical claim is contrary to the Christian faith, it is an abuse of philosophy resulting from a failure of reason.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Natural reason can demonstratively prove the existence of God.

claimsPreview.text: Natural reason can demonstratively prove the existence of God.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Anselm’s ontological argument lacks force as a proof for God's existence.

claimsPreview.text: Anselm’s ontological argument lacks force as a proof for God's existence.

mismatchSamples.goldText: An infinite series of simultaneous causes is impossible.

claimsPreview.text: An infinite series of simultaneous causes is impossible.

mismatchSamples.goldText: The first cause of the world is entirely simple and non-composite.

claimsPreview.text: The first cause of the world is entirely simple and non-composite.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Natural reason is incapable of establishing the triune nature of God.

claimsPreview.text: Natural reason is incapable of establishing the triune nature of God.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Divine revelation is necessary for human salvation.

claimsPreview.text: Divine revelation is necessary for human salvation.

mismatchSamples.goldText: It is not possible for human beings to know what God is, but only what God is not.

claimsPreview.text: It is not possible for human beings to know what God is, but only what God is not.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Predicates applied to both God and creatures are used analogically rather than univocally or equivocally.

claimsPreview.text: Predicates applied to both God and creatures are used analogically rather than univocally or equivocally.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Creatures possess goodness only by participation in the goodness of God.

claimsPreview.text: Creatures possess goodness only by participation in the goodness of God.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Creation in the strict sense is the act of bringing a thing into existence from nothing (ex nihilo).

claimsPreview.text: Creation in the strict sense is the act of bringing a thing into existence from nothing (ex nihilo).

mismatchSamples.goldText: Only God has the power to create something ex nihilo.

claimsPreview.text: Only God has the power to create something ex nihilo.

mismatchSamples.goldText: No demonstrative argument can prove whether the world is eternal or had a beginning in time.

claimsPreview.text: No demonstrative argument can prove whether the world is eternal or had a beginning in time.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Defending the faith through dubious attempts at philosophical demonstration discredits the faith.

claimsPreview.text: Defending the faith through dubious attempts at philosophical demonstration discredits the faith.

mismatchSamples.goldText: Space cannot exist without the existence of bodies.

claimsPreview.text: Space cannot exist without the existence of bodies.
